content.full_text:
January 10, 1978

A-78-01-023

Mr. James P. Greene
Pacific Lighting Corporation
P.O. Box 60043, Terminal Annex
Los Angeles, California 90060

Dear Mr. Greene:

In your letter of December 13, 1977, you ask whether amendments to Government
Code Section 82013 by Chapter 1213 of the 1977 Statutes, effective January 1,
1978, render unnecessary the filing of a semi-annual campaign statement for
the last half of 1977. You note that under the prior language of 82013 you
were a Committee in 1977 but under the new language you would not have been.

The filing obligation in Section 84206, requiring semi-annual statements, is
incurred by reason of financial activity during the specified periods. During
the last six months of 1977 you were a Committee under the terms of 82013 then
in effect. During that same period you had financial activity. Therefore,
84206 requires you to report for the six month period, all of which is in 1977.
The fact that the report is not due to be submitted until 1978 does not affect
your obligation to report the 1977 activity under the law in effect in 1977.

Sincerely yours,

John D. Giansello
Political Analyst
Technical Assistance and
Analysis Division

19

PACIFIC LIGHTING CORPORATION

James P. Greene
Attorney

Mailing Address
810 South Flower Street
Los Angeles, California 90017
Telephone (213) 689-3341

P.O. Box 60043
Terminal Annex
Los Angeles, California 90060

December 13, 1977

Executive Director
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K. Street Building
Sacramento, California

Re: Request for Written Advice
(Cal. Gov. Code § 83114(b))

Pursuant to the provisions of California Government Code
Section 83114(b) and 2 California Administrative Code
§ 18329, I hereby request the Commission's advice on the
question presented herein.

This request is hereby presented on behalf of Southern
California Gas Company (the "Company"), a California
corporation.

During calendar year 1977 the Company has not made any
contributions or expenditures which are reportable under
the provisions of the Political Reform Act of 1974,
except that it has provided certain administrative
support to a committee which is a "separate segregated
fund" (as that term is used at 2 U.S.C. § 441b)
"political committee" (as that term is used at 2 U.S.C.
§ 431) and which is "affiliated" (as that term is used at
2 U.S.C. § 433 and 11 C.F.R. § 102.2(b)) with the
Company. For your information the affiliated committee
which receives the Company's administrative support has
in the past, and will continue to report the value of
that support as a contribution.

During 1977 the Company, solely as a result of the
activity described above, became a committee as defined
by Government Code Section 82013(b). Therefore it filed
a semi-annual campaign statement for the period January
1, 1977 through June 30, 1977 during July, 1977.

Executive Director
December 13, 1977
Page 2


Subsequent to the filing of the above-described semi-
annual campaign statement, the California Legislature
enacted AB 1730 (Chapter No. 1213, 1977-1978 Regular
Session). My understanding of that legislation is that
it amended the definition of "committee" at Government
Code Section 82013 so that a person only making "in kind
contributions" must make $5000 of such contributions per
year, rather than $500 as Section 82013(b) currently
provides, in order to be a "committee" and therefore be
obligated to file campaign statements. Furthermore, I
understand that Chapter No. 1213 will be effective on
January 1, 1978.

It is currently anticipated that the value of the
administrative support the Company is providing the
affiliated separate segregated fund political committee
will be somewhere between $500 and $5,000 for calendar
year 1977. Thus, under the present definition of
"committee" it is required to file campaign statements
but under the definition which will be effective on
January 1, 1978 it would not be required to file the
statements.

According to Government Code Section 84206 the next
semi-annual statement due shall cover the period of
July 1 through December 31, a period of time in which the
existing definition of "committee" is applicable.
However, that statement may be filed not later than
January 31, 1978, after Chapter 1213 becomes effective.

In view of the above, the Company requests the
Commission's advice as to whether it is required to file
a semi-annual statement for the period July 1, 1977 -
December 31, 1977, assuming that it is unable to file
that statement until sometime in January, 1978.

If you have any questions concerning this request, please
do not hesitate to contact me.

Thank you very much for your assistance.

Sincerely,

James P. Greene

JPG:cjm

sections.conclusion: During the first half of 1977 you were a committee under the terms of Section 82013. During the same period you had financial activity. This requires you to file a statement covering that activity for the one-month period, all of which occurred in 1977. Your obligation to file is not determined by when you are required to submit it. The filing obligation is based on your status in 1977. You are therefore required to file a semi-annual statement for 1977 activity under the law in effect in 1977.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether Southern California Gas Company must file a semi-annual campaign statement for July 1 - December 31, 1977, when the statement cannot be filed until after January 1, 1978, given that new legislation effective January 1, 1978 changes the definition of 'committee' and would exempt the company from filing requirements if it provided less than $5,000 in in-kind contributions during 2017.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The company must file the semi-annual statement for July 1 - December 31, 1977, because the filing obligation is determined by the status and activity during the period covered (1977) under the law in effect at that time, not by when the statement is actually submitted or by the law in effect when the statement is filed.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: modern; LLM: Document contains both the incoming request letter (dated December 13, 1977) and the FPPC response letter (dated January 10, 1978). Response section is somewhat degraded in image quality but content is discernible. Question and conclusion sections extracted from both documents respectively.

embedding.summary: FPPC responds to Southern California Gas Company's request for advice on whether it must file a semi-annual campaign statement for July 1 - December 31, 1977, when legislative amendments effective January 1, 1978 would change the definition of 'committee.' FPPC advises that the filing obligation is based on the company's status and activities in 1977 under the law then in effect, requiring the statement to be filed.
